Function: Main hub for Southeast Regional Climate Center data, tools, and publications.
User Segment: Researchers, state climatologists, planners, ag extension agents, emergency managers
Impact Evidence: Central SERCC hub; referenced in SE state climatologist workflows across AL, FL, GA, NC, SC, VA, PR, VI
Notes: Primary SERCC portal hosted at UNC-CH; ACIS-backed; stable institutional home

Function: Primary interface for station and climate division data (temperature, precipitation, SPI).
User Segment: Researchers, state climatologists, data analysts, NWS forecasters
Impact Evidence: Referenced in SE state drought monitoring and agricultural situation reports
Notes: Primary ACIS data interface for SERCC region; station and climate division data access

Function: State-level climate summaries and analyses for Southeast states.
User Segment: Planners, policymakers, researchers, educators, media
Impact Evidence: Referenced in SE state hazard mitigation plans and climate adaptation frameworks
Notes: State-level climate summaries; NCEI has national equivalents but SERCC provides SE-specific depth and framing

Function: Interactive dashboard with current temperature, precipitation, and anomalies.
User Segment: Researchers, state agencies, emergency managers, media, planners
Notes: Interactive regional monitoring dashboard; ACIS-backed; no direct SE-specific substitute

Function: Form for custom or bulk data requests.
User Segment: Researchers, planners, data analysts
Notes: Custom data request form; administrative tool; low standalone restoration priority

Function: Automated daily/weekly climate summaries, extremes, and rankings for stations in the Southeast.
User Segment: Researchers, state climatologists, NWS forecasters, media, planners
Impact Evidence: Embedded in SE state climatologist daily monitoring workflows; referenced in regional climate communication materials
Notes: Climate Perspectives is SERCC's most distinctive monitoring product — automated daily/weekly summaries with extremes and rankings; no equivalent automated reporting tool exists at other RCCs or nationally at this regional specificity

Function: Monthly and seasonal summaries for the Southeast, including anomaly maps.
User Segment: Planners, state agencies, researchers, media
Notes: Monthly and seasonal summaries; staff-authored narrative component limits automated restoration; maps are ACIS-recoverable

Function: Explore historical climate data and trends by state or station.
User Segment: Researchers, planners, educators, state climatologists
Notes: Historical data browser by state and station; ACIS-backed; CLIMOD2 and cli-MATE are partial substitutes at other centers

Function: Displays current drought conditions, SPI maps, and precipitation deficits.
User Segment: Emergency managers, water resource managers, farmers, state agencies
Impact Evidence: Feeds into SE state drought contingency plan workflows
Notes: Regional drought access portal; NDMC is partial national substitute; SERCC version provides SE-specific SPI maps and precipitation deficit framing

Function: Calculates WBGT values for user-selected stations or grid cells; includes thresholds for occupational heat safety.
User Segment: Public health agencies, emergency managers, outdoor workers, military, athletic trainers, utilities, municipal planners
Impact Evidence: Referenced in SE state occupational health guidance and military installation heat safety protocols; used by OSHA regional offices in SE
Notes: WBGT tool is SERCC's most unique and high-impact product — occupational and athletic heat safety thresholds at station level; no national equivalent integrating ACIS station data with WBGT calculation at this resolution; critical for SE population given high heat exposure and outdoor labor sectors

Function: Interactive heat-health monitoring portal integrating temp, humidity, and NWS heat advisories.
Data Source: ACIS, NWS heat advisories, humidity data
User Segment: Public health agencies, emergency managers, municipal planners, media, utilities
Impact Evidence: Referenced in FL and GA state public health heat emergency response plans; used by SE county health departments
Notes: Heat & Health Dashboard integrates temperature, humidity, and NWS advisories into a regional monitoring portal; no national equivalent at SE station resolution; highest public health impact tool in SERCC catalog

Function: Visualizes precipitation trends and anomalies over selectable time periods.
User Segment: Hydrologists, planners, researchers, water resource managers
Notes: Precipitation trend visualization; ACIS-backed; NOAA Climate Explorer partial substitute

Function: Displays recurrence intervals for heavy rainfall events by station or climate division.
User Segment: Stormwater engineers, municipal planners, floodplain managers, FEMA staff
Impact Evidence: Referenced in SE state stormwater design standards and FEMA floodplain management guidance
Notes: Rainfall return period tool; NOAA Atlas 14 exists nationally but SERCC provides SE-specific usable interface; high infrastructure planning value

Function: Maps and charts historical rainfall extremes and percentile rankings.
User Segment: Hydrologists, emergency managers, researchers, floodplain managers
Notes: Extreme precipitation mapping and percentile rankings; NCEI has national data; SERCC interface provides SE-specific usability

Function: News and highlights on notable weather and climate events.
User Segment: Researchers, media, general public, educators
Notes: Climate blog; static content; archivable; low restoration priority

Function: Teaching materials and climate literacy resources.
User Segment: K-12 educators, students, general public
Notes: Climate literacy materials; many national substitutes; low unique restoration value

Function: Reports and technical summaries published by SERCC scientists.
User Segment: Researchers, planners, policymakers
Impact Evidence: SERCC technical publications cited in SE state climate assessments
Notes: Publications archive; static content; archivable as-is; high archival value even if low restoration priority

Function: Decision-support tools for farmers in the Southeast (GDDs, chill hours, crop stress, drought risk).
User Segment: SE farmers, ag extension agents, crop consultants, irrigation managers
Impact Evidence: AgroClimate cited in FL, GA, SC cooperative extension crop management guides; embedded in SE row crop and specialty crop workflows
Notes: AgroClimate is an affiliate tool hosted at agroclimate.org rather than sercc.com — institutional dependency is outside SERCC's direct control; GDD, chill hours, crop stress, and drought risk tools unique to SE agriculture; no national equivalent at this crop and regional specificity

Function: Map of current and historical frost and freeze occurrences across the Southeast.
User Segment: Farmers, ag extension agents, growers, crop consultants
Notes: Frost and freeze occurrence mapping for SE; ACIS-backed; MRCC VIP suite is partial substitute for Midwest but SE version serves distinct crop calendar and geography

Function: Full archived interface prior to current redesign (ACIS data, reports, perspectives).
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Full legacy portal archive; Wayback captures exist; mine for discontinued tool inventory

Function: Original version of Climate Perspectives tool.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Original Climate Perspectives interface; superseded by current version; archival interest only

Function: Early drought portal predating new monitor integration.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Early drought portal; superseded by current drought monitor integration; archival interest only

Function: Beta version of WBGT calculator and health mapping tool.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Beta WBGT and health mapping prototype; superseded by current WBGT and Heat & Health tools; useful for methodology lineage

Function: Legacy technical documents and reports.
User Segment: Researchers, policymakers
Notes: Pre-2022 technical publications; archivable as-is; verify overlap with current publications archive
